title: Security Driver Functions
text: Security Driver Functions A security driver must perform the action requested of it, as defined by the Function member of the USecDrv structure. Some actions are applicable to both client/server and SMTP security drivers, others are specific to one or the other. Table 1. Actions Specified by the Function Member Function Description Client/Server SMTP USEC_DRVINFO Specify this security driver's functionality. X X USEC_AUTHENTICATE Authenticate the username and password combination. X USEC_CLOSE Release any resources the driver acquired. X X USEC_USERPASSINFO Provide authentication information, such as "Your password will expire in x days" Copy X USEC_PASSCHNG Change the password for a given user. X USEC_ENCODE Encode the contents of InBuf and place the result in OutBuf . X USEC_DECODE Decode the contents of InBuf and place the result in OutBuf . X USEC_VERIFY Indicate whether the specified ESMTP extension is supported. X USEC_OPENMSG Prepare the messages that are to be used in the initialisation and authentication protocol to establish a connection. X USEC_CLOSEMSG Prepare the messages that are used in the disconnection protocol. X USEC_RTINFO Return runtime information about negotiation options. X Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
